title: qwen: web tool calls fail with "params must have required property 'prompt'/'pattern'" (v0.297.1)
body:
## Summary

On **v0.297.1**, running `formal-ai with qwen` and asking for any web tool (fetch/search) makes the tool **fire**, but the tool executor rejects the call with a parameter-schema mismatch. The qwen path expects parameter names `prompt`/`pattern`, but the request carries `url`/`query`, so every tool call fails at execution while the *routing* succeeds.

This is qwen-CLI-specific: `opencode` and `agent` execute the identical fetch cleanly on the same running server.

## Environment

- `formal-ai 0.297.1` (installed via `cargo install formal-ai`)
- Server: `formal-ai serve --port 8080 --agent-mode`
- qwen CLI present on PATH
- macOS (Darwin arm64)

## Reproduction

```
formal-ai serve --port 8080 --agent-mode &

formal-ai with --non-interactive --no-start-server qwen "fetch https://example.com"
# => Fetched `https://example.com`. Response body:
#    ```text
#    params must have required property 'prompt'
#    ```

formal-ai with --non-interactive --no-start-server qwen "search the web for the capital of France"
# => params must have required property 'pattern'
```

Plain (non-tool) prompts work fine on qwen:

```
formal-ai with --non-interactive --no-start-server qwen "hi"
# => Hi, how may I help you?
```

## Expected

The web tool executes and returns the page/search result — as it does for `opencode`:

```
formal-ai with --non-interactive --no-start-server opencode "fetch https://example.com"
# => Fetched `https://example.com`. Response body: Example Domain...
```

## Likely cause

The tool arguments emitted for the qwen protocol namespace use a different parameter name than the executor validates against (`prompt`/`pattern` vs `url`/`query`). The advertised tool schema and the executor's expected schema are out of sync on the qwen path.


---
## Acceptance (all tools, all languages)
Honoring the advertised schema (param names + all required fields) must be verified for **every** tool each CLI advertises (~29 total, see #758) and across **all supported/added natural languages** (en/ru/hi/zh + #706, at parity). Same root cause as #749/#755/#758; generalization mandate #745. E2e via real TUIs — #671. (Verification sweep excludes claude, though claude `-p` may be used for spot checks.)


---
## Implementation spec (qwen concrete required fields — apply via #758 §B)
qwen advertises (captured): `web_fetch{required:[url,prompt]}`, `grep_search{required:[pattern]}`, shell tool `{required:[…,is_background]}`, `read_file{required:[absolute_path]}`. The argument builder must, for qwen:
- web_fetch → `{url:<url>, prompt:<the user's request/derived instruction>}` (prompt was missing).
- web_search intent → `grep_search`? No — qwen has no web_search; map web-search intent to qwen's `web_fetch`+search or advertise none. For code search → `grep_search{pattern:<term>}` (was sent `{query}`).
- shell → include `is_background:false` plus the command in qwen's declared key.
- read_file → `{absolute_path:<abs path>}` (resolve to absolute).
Never feed the validation error back as stdout (#750 §3).
